title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated Epoch Ownership Follow-On Work Item
summary: Trace and prove the unbounded repeated epoch ownership model that keeps current, next, and retained-old 1-RTT state distinct while repeated updates remain active.
planned_changes:
- Make the repeated epoch ownership model explicit instead of relying on representative later-epoch cleanup alone.
- Keep current, next, and retained-old 1-RTT ownership separate in the `QuicOneRttKeyUpdateLifecycle` seam.
- Preserve current-phase acknowledgment and repeated-update cooldown ownership when retained-old material is discarded.
- Keep sender/recovery cleanup synchronized with retained-old discard.
- Preserve the representative later-epoch cleanup benchmark and fuzz/property coverage as bounded evidence only.
- Keep broader stateless-reset response behavior outside this follow-on.
out_of_scope: Broader stateless-reset response behavior outside the retained-route floor
Token generation or token storage changes
verification_plan: Run the representative later-epoch requirement-home tests and the repeated old-key cleanup benchmark cases, add any missing negative or fuzz coverage if the ownership model exposes a new seam, render the touched SpecTrace Markdown views from JSON, and finish with git diff --check.
completion_notes: Planned. The current runtime only proves representative later-epoch cleanup across phases 6 through 32; this follow-on exists to make the unbounded repeated epoch ownership model explicit and keep sender/recovery cleanup traceable.
